task: Evaluated architecture and conflict-resolution strategy for multi-user adoption; decided to generate product_brief.md dynamically on git pull rather than committing it to avoid divergence conflicts.
discussion_summary: Session focused on adoption readiness and file conflict patterns. User identified that shared files (decisions.md, goals.md, etc.) create race conditions when multiple co-founders push simultaneously. Discussed whether product_brief.md should be committed or generated dynamically. User rejected static committed brief in favor of in-memory generation on pull to eliminate conflicts. Also clarified that architecture.md should become the comprehensive tracking document, replacing product_brief.md's role in the roadmap.

accomplishments.what: Identified root cause of multi-user conflicts: shared mutable state files (decisions.md, goals.md, notifications.log) with sequential push assumptions
Validated daemon multi-project monitoring is working correctly with independent per-project cooldowns
Confirmed no tweaks needed to current daemon implementation

next_actions.action: Design product_brief.md generation logic: create a script that runs on `git pull` to synthesize current state from architecture.md, goals.md, and decisions.md into a fresh brief without committing it
next_actions.why: User explicitly rejected static committed brief; dynamic generation eliminates merge conflicts and keeps brief in memory only

next_actions.action: Audit architecture.md for comprehensiveness: ensure it can serve as the single source of truth for roadmap phases and project structure, replacing product_brief.md's tracking role
next_actions.why: User indicated architecture.md should become the comprehensive tracking document; need to verify it has sufficient detail for all phases

next_actions.action: Design conflict-resolution strategy for shared mutable files: implement file-level locking or last-write-wins with conflict markers for decisions.md and goals.md when co-founders push within minutes
next_actions.why: User identified race conditions and compounding conflicts as blocker to adoption; current sequential assumption breaks under real multi-user load

next_actions.action: Map adoption priority: determine whether askr or leaps should be built first for co-founder sync, based on which project has higher dependency on shared state
next_actions.why: User asked which project should be prioritized for adoption; answer depends on conflict resolution strategy and shared file dependencies

decisions.decision: product_brief.md will be generated dynamically on git pull, not committed to git
decisions.reason: Eliminates merge conflicts and keeps brief in memory; faster and easier to understand without divergence

decisions.decision: architecture.md will become the comprehensive tracking document for roadmap phases
decisions.reason: User indicated this should replace product_brief.md's role; reduces file fragmentation

user_rejected_decisions.what_was_proposed: Commit product_brief.md to git as a static tracked file
user_rejected_decisions.user_signal: I agree with the generate project brief.md on git pull, as that makes more sense, faster and easier to understand, not commited to git, so stays in memory and updates without conflicts

failed_approaches.approach: Assumed minimal conflicts on shared files with sequential push model
failed_approaches.reason: User observed actual race conditions and compounding conflicts when co-founders push within minutes; sequential assumption does not hold under real adoption

relational_files.why: Will become source of truth for roadmap phases and project structure; product_brief.md will be generated from it
Shared mutable state; source of conflicts when multiple users edit simultaneously
Shared mutable state; source of conflicts when multiple users edit simultaneously
Validated multi-project monitoring and per-project cooldowns are working correctly

blockers: Multi-user conflict resolution strategy needed for shared mutable files (decisions.md, goals.md) before adoption with co-founder can proceed safely
